title: Race-Archetype Mixture Of Experts Using Undercut/Overcut Regimes
summary: Official F1 strategy coverage highlights that pit timing value changes by degradation regime and circuit behavior: high-degradation tracks favor undercuts, while low-degradation tracks can favor overcuts.
rationale: Official F1 strategy coverage highlights that pit timing value changes by degradation regime and circuit behavior: high-degradation tracks favor undercuts, while low-degradation tracks can favor overcuts. A single global model may blur these race archetypes.
implementation_hint: Build race-level train-only priors such as average degradation growth, lap-time drop-off by compound, and historical same-race pit timing distributions, then cluster or threshold races into 2-4 archetypes like high-deg dry, low-deg dry, and wet/intermediate. Train separate experts per archetype and use a simple gate from current-race observable features.
expected_effect: Should help when the same tyre-age or pace-loss pattern means different pit urgency on Suzuka-style low-deg races versus Singapore-style high-deg races.
risk: Archetypes can overfragment the data, and any race prior computed using full-race information from the validation fold is leakage.
